title: petradoria pumila
intro: Petradoria pumila, also known as prairie pink, is a flowering plant native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Its health benefits include reducing pain and swelling, as well as treating skin conditions such as acne and eczema. The plant's roots and leaves can be used to prepare herbal remedies, including teas, salves, and infusions.
